COMMODITY TRADING: Vitol Group, the world’s largest oil trading house, saw profits roughly halve to around $4.5 billion in 2025. 

Despite the fall, last year would still rank as one of Vitol’s best years on record. Before 2020, its profits had never exceeded $3 billion.
@1therhino Agree. The old MS physical machine would have done rather well on the current market dislocation. Imagine the money they would have made in US refined products alone.

The question in the future would be: should Japan return to its pre-war oil import pattern, taking Strait of Hormuz risk? Persian Gulf nations without bypass capacity (that's you, Kuwait, Iraq and Qatar) would face an uphill battle in Tokyo, for sure.

quoted.text:
🇮🇷🇺🇸 Blow-by-blow account from the IRGC-linked @FarsNews_Agency of how we got to this point:

▫️At the same time as the US tactically retreats from new additions to the draft agreement, Donald Trump is trying to create a narrative of "Iran's surrender in the face of bombing" by increasing his threatening tone on social media; a narrative that is far from the reality on the ground of the negotiations.

▫️According to information from our correspondent, about two weeks ago, the text of the draft MOU between the negotiating teams was almost finalized and was only awaiting final approval in Tehran and Washington. However, during this review, Trump once again demanded several new details be added, contrary to the agreement of the American negotiators who had accepted the Iranian draft. In response, Iran announced that it would not review the new text and practically did not respond to the US.

▫️After that, scattered tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, in southern Iran, and the attack on Dahiyeh, practically silenced the negotiation file.

▫️But since Wednesday, the Qatari team entered the arena through mediation and announced the US withdrawal from the new additional clauses; that is, a return to the original text that was still awaiting final approval in Iran.

▫️At the same time as this noticeable US withdrawal, Trump launched a media campaign and threatening rhetoric to suggest that Iran has backed down under the pressure of bombing. But the reality is that up to this point, not only has Iran not given a final response, but it is the US that has returned to its previous demand.

▫️Of course, it seems that given that the US has accepted the text proposed by Iran, there is now a possibility of re-examining this text.

quoted.text:
Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, President Trump claimed an agreement on Iran has been reached, and a deal will be signed, likely in Europe, as soon as this weekend.

Iran has not confirmed any such agreement.

"We just made a great settlement of the war with Iran," Mr. Trump said. "And we're going to be, subject to finalization of documents, we should get done over the next few days. Probably have a signing, maybe in Europe. And it's a great thing. Stock market's up a thousand points. That means they like the deal."

The president said the deal means Iran will never have a nuclear weapon.

"We have a signing soon," Mr. Trump said. "And the documents are in pretty final shape. So we'll see. We'll see. Very good. It should be done. That should be done pretty quickly. They want it every bit as much as everybody else wants it."

The president said he just spoke with leaders of a number of relevant countries including Israel, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain and others. The president said he'll soon be talking to Turkey, too.

Mr. Trump said he won't be present for a signing, but Vice President JD Vance will be.

The president said the Strait of Hormuz will be opened as soon as the "settlement" is signed. https://t.co/NIL77j95Pz

MOJTABA KHAMENEI REJECTS U.S. PEACE DEAL: IRANIAN LEADERSHIP REPORTEDLY DIVIDED

Reports indicate Iran’s Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, will not approve the proposed peace deal with the United States.

Sources claim Iranian negotiators signaled the deal was ready, but the decision was reportedly announced by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi without Khamenei’s approval.

If accurate, it raises serious questions about whether mediators were misled regarding Iran’s true position. 🇮🇷🇺🇸
